title: As alcohol consumption drops, Chilean wineries beckon younger drinkers

title: Uruguay declares wine a living culture in landmark policy move
summary: Uruguay has formally recognised wine as a ‘living culture’ through a cross-government initiative designed to integrate cultural policy, industry strategy and international diplomacy. The programme aims to redefine the country’s wine sector as a broader expression of heritage, identity and community.
content: Uruguay has formally recognised wine as a ‘living culture’ through a cross-government initiative designed to integrate cultural policy, industry strategy and international diplomacy. The programme aims to redefine the country’s wine sector as a broader expression of heritage, identity and community.

title: Pernod Ricard and Brown-Forman Abandon Merger Talks, Both Confirm
summary: Pernod Ricard and Brown-Forman are no longer pursuing a potential deal, both companies confirmed in separate press releases today. Brown-Forman’s stock fell by 5.08 percent as of 4:54 p.m. in after-hours trading following the announcements. News that discussions have been terminated comes just over a month after Pernod and Brown-Forman’s will-they-won’t-they saga came to light. In late March, both spirits producers confirmed they were exploring a business deal, which they described as a “merger of equals.” Sazerac threw its hat into the ring when it offered to buy Brown-Forman for $15 billion.

title: Premium Napa Valley Crystal Springs Grapes Available
summary: Premium Crystal Springs Napa Valley Grapes Available For the upcoming 2026 harvest, we will have premium Tempranillo, Grenache and Touriga Nacional grapes are available. In an ocean of Cabernet Sauvignon, these varietals thrive and give today's Napa Valley consumers something special and truly unique.  At the base of the steep, volcanic slopes of Howell Mountain lies Napa Valley’s newest and highly distinctive sub-appellation, the Crystal Springs AVA. Here, the renowned VIADER Vineyards & Winery estate has been family-owned and meticulously farmed for nearly four decades, producing wines that consistently earn 96–98+ point critical acclaim. These exceptional hillside vineyards are densely planted along dramatic slopes, with an east–west orientation that maximizes sun exposure while preserving freshness and structure. The vines are hand-farmed, sustainably cultivated, and Napa Green certified, reflecting the estate’s long-standing commitment to thoughtful stewardship and precision viticulture. Availability:  3+ tons of Tempranillo (Clone 770/1103-P) 3+ tons of Grenache (Clone 362/110R) 3+ tons of Touriga Nacional (Clone 5/1103-P) Farming: Hand-farmed, sustainable, Napa Green certified Site: Steep volcanic hillside within Crystal Springs AVA Pricing: Napa Valley average. Negotiable.  Fruit from this site consistently delivers intensity, mountain structure, and remarkable depth, ideal for producers seeking world-class Napa Valley grapes. Serious inquiries welcome.
